tool_args.label: LIRR & Metro-North Key Performance Metrics - May 2024

payload.title: LONG ISLAND RAIL ROAD & METRO-NORTH RAILROAD KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.prepared_for: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committees

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Comprised of over 7,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day, operating 700 trains daily, maintaining 125 stations, nearly 700 miles of track, and 27 shops and yards.
Comprised of nearly 6,000 employees serving over 200,000 passengers a day, operating 700 trains daily, maintaining 124 stations, nearly 900 miles of track, and 19 shops and yards.

payload.entity_name: Joint Long Island Rail Road & Metro-North Railroad Committee

tool_args.label: Quemuel Arroyo, MTA Chief Accessibility Officer

payload.description: Completion of upgrades to Metro-North's maintenance and operations hub at Croton-on-Hudson. A 400,000 square-foot facility employing 1,200 people, executed in five phases since 2001. Features repair and support shops, a dozen car hoists, four pedestal tracks, and multiple door-level and roof-level platforms for preventive maintenance.

Rehabilitation of the West Side Storage Yard damaged during Superstorm Sandy. Included replacement of heavy rail systems, CCTV, PA systems, radio base stations, fire alarm components, fiber optic cable panels, third rail infrastructure. The yard contains 30 tracks and is the biggest storage yard on the West side of Manhattan.

MTA Construction & Development transforming Brewster Yard into an efficient maintenance facility and transportation hub serving the Harlem Line. Divided into three phases: Southeast Station Parking and Access Improvements, Northward Yard Expansion, and Existing Yard Reconfiguration. Phase 1 includes a five-level 1,320 space parking garage, rooftop solar, new waiting room, intermodal areas, and two bridges for pedestrian and vehicular connections.

Weekend service pilot on the Haverstraw-Ossining ferry between Memorial Day and Veterans Day, operating continuously on weekends from 9:30 a.m. to 9:30 p.m., extending beyond usual weekday rush-hour schedule. Also includes monthly UniTicket discounts of 78-90% on ferries and buses crossing the Hudson River.

Discount program running on Saturdays between July 6 and August 31, 2024. Monthly pass holders can travel to any destination on the railroad that issued their monthly pass, with up to two additional people, for $1 per person. For LIRR: each Saturday in July and August, monthly ticket holders can travel anywhere in LIRR service territory and bring up to two people for $1.00 Family Fare per leg.
Grade crossing renewal at Commack Road on the Main Line just west of Deer Park Station. The project spanned two weekends and will lead to a smoother ride for both trains and cars.

tool_args.label: MTA secured funding for West of Hudson fare pilots

payload.claim_text: Thanks to support from Governor Kathy Hochul and the State Legislature, MTA secured funding to enhance the reach of Metro-North Railroad to Orange and Rockland Counties as an alternative to driving into New York City. The pilot program includes beginning weekend service on the Haverstraw-Ossining ferries for the first time ever, creating monthly UniTicket discounts of 78-90% on ferries and buses, and slashing parking rates by 25% for Orange and Rockland residents using Metro-North's parking garage in North White Plains.

tool_args.label: LIRR accessibility progress - one step closer to accessible stations

payload.claim_text: The Long Island Rail Road moved one step closer to having accessibility at all 126 of our stations with the grand debut of the new elevator at Copiague Station on May 16. Two separate ADA station project bundles currently underway. Copiague is the first of four stations to be completed in 2024. Amityville and Lindenhurst will follow very soon and Massapequa Park later in the summer. Once all active ADA station projects are done, only seven more to go until every LIRR station is fully accessible.

tool_args.label: Four major incidents affected Metro-North OTP in April 2024

payload.claim_text: Four major incidents negatively affected April's OTP: On April 3, multiple downed trees on our right-of-way caused delays throughout the operating territory, impacting 45 trains. On April 8, a train was unable to take power affecting our morning rush hour service, affecting 18 trains. On April 19, a trespasser near the Appalachian Trail station affected service on the upper Harlem Line, impacting 10 trains. On April 25, a switch failure at CP109 delayed trains between CP106 and CP112, affecting 24 trains.

tool_args.label: Non-commutation ridership surpassed 2019 for 8th consecutive month

payload.claim_text: Non-commutation ridership surpassed the same month in 2019 for the 8th consecutive month – this time by 11.4%
